verdicts.rationale: standalone-install-and-trigger.md:14-22 states the standalone version 'consists of one (huge) program which contains all the needed code', 'executed via transaction SE38', and is 'meant to be installed and run' before abapGit is present (clause completes on line 23, immediately adjacent to the cited range). Matches the sentence; no conflict with the L1 page, which independently corroborates the SE38/interactive entry path.

abapgit-purpose-and-actor.md:14-20 explicitly states abapGit 'is a git client for ABAP developed in ABAP' and that its business value is putting artifacts under version control in plain text 'which enables code review before importing changes into a target system.' Sentence is a direct restatement.

abapgit-purpose-and-actor.md:22-26 states verbatim that the audience is 'ABAP developers, not business/end users' and that abapGit is 'a cross-cutting developer tool, not part of any FI/MM/SD/CO business process.'

deployment-context-owner.md:17-26 states the object was 'NOT productively installed and has NEVER been executed in this system' and that the ZABAPGIT package/TADIR row are 'a synthetic demo fixture'; l1-code-analysis-facts.md:21-24 cites the L1 page's used_by:[] / 'no known references'. Both consistent with the L1 wiki page frontmatter (used_by: []) and 'Where used' section (no known references). No contradiction with code.

standalone-install-and-trigger.md:24-28 states the report is 'a user-invoked developer tool launched interactively from SE38; the docs describe no scheduled-job trigger.' Direct match; consistent with L1's interactive/CALL SELECTION-SCREEN 1001 entry path (no self-scheduling logic documented in L1 either).

l1-code-analysis-facts.md:14-19 states the batch path (sy-batch -> zcl_abapgit_background=>run()) and that 'the report shell contains no self-scheduling logic'. This is itself a summary of the L1 wiki page's own 'open_gui' FORM analysis (if sy-batch -> zcl_abapgit_background=>run()), so it is fully consistent with the code, not just the evidence file.
deployment-context-owner.md:17-26 states verbatim 'No background or scheduled job launches it... it is never triggered here.' Matches sentence exactly.
abapgit-purpose-and-actor.md:22-26 states the audience is 'developers with system access... ABAP developers, not business/end users.' Direct match.

Status is 'inferred' with no cited evidence, and the sentence itself correctly flags this as standard SAP knowledge rather than source-proven. Per the inferred/not-verifiable rule this is capped at partially_supported: the S_DEVELOP authorization claim is a reasonable, plausible inference (also consistent with the L1 page's own BUG-002 mention of 'S_DEVELOP') but is not proven by any cited source, exactly as labelled.

standalone-install-and-trigger.md:14-22 states the standalone version 'consists of one (huge) program which contains all the needed code... executed via transaction SE38' and the developer version is 'targeted at developers contributing to the abapGit codebase', run via transaction ZABAPGIT, and 'requires installing the standalone version first.' Full match.

The cited ranges (standalone-install-and-trigger.md:35-37 and l1-code-analysis-facts.md:26-29) do support that the version constant/merge are 'packaging facts, not business rules' and that the self-reported version is '1.133.0, produced by abapmerge 0.16.8'. However neither cited file (checked in full) contains any mention of an 'About page' - that detail is present only in the L1 wiki page prose ('prints its own version in the "About" page'), which is not among the cited evidence for this claim. The core assertion is proven; the 'About page' detail is not proven by the cited sources.

l1-code-analysis-facts.md:26-32 states the object has 'no <COMPANY> business logic to document here'. 'No magic numbers / hardcoded business IFs to interpret' is a reasonable restatement of 'no business logic' in the business_rules section's standard framing, not a separate unproven fact; consistent with the L1 page's own executive summary ('there is no <COMPANY> business logic to document here').

standalone-install-and-trigger.md:30-33 supports BASIS 702+, SAP GUI for Windows, SSL for online features, and 'Package recommendation: use a local $ package (e.g. $ABAPGIT)'. The word 'dedicated' is not established by this cited range - the 'do not reuse a package with other objects' nuance that would justify 'dedicated' appears only in online-offline-usage-integration.md (not cited here). Core prerequisites are proven; the 'dedicated' qualifier is not proven by the cited evidence.

online-offline-usage-integration.md:14-21 describes the online workflow (transaction ZABAPGIT, clone, commit, push, pull) and states 'abapGit synchronizes the SAP system against a remote Git repo. This is the transport-independent object exchange abapGit provides.' Direct match; describes abapGit generically (as the evidence does), not a false claim about this specific object's own tcode.

online-offline-usage-integration.md:23-31 states offline repos let developers work with 'no internet and no SSL certificates... the "offline installation and usage" theme of this slice', set up via 'New Offline', and objects 'imported via ZIP upload, and exported as ZIP'. Direct match.

l1-code-analysis-facts.md:26-32 states touchpoints are 'all standard SAP: repository/dictionary tables (TADIR, E070/E071, REPOSRC, TRDIR) and dynpro function modules - not custom Z tables.' Matches; consistent with the L1 page's own 'External dependencies' table list (TADIR, E070, E071, REPOSRC, TRDIR, RPY_DYNPRO_*, RS_SET_SELSCREEN_STATUS).
online-offline-usage-integration.md:33-37 states abapGit 'serializes them to plain-text Git files (online) or ZIP files (offline)' and 'does not populate <COMPANY> business Z tables'. Direct match.

l1-code-analysis-facts.md:26-32 supports the read-side touchpoints (TADIR, E070/E071, REPOSRC, TRDIR) and 'not custom Z tables'. The clause 'abapGit's own repository metadata lives in its internal persistence classes' is not stated anywhere in the cited file (checked in full) - that phrasing ('its persistence is abapGit's own repo metadata') appears only in online-offline-usage-integration.md:33-37, which is not cited for this claim. Table list is proven; the persistence-classes detail is not proven by the cited evidence.
